Amended DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-18-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Ariella Mehrzadi, Esq., SHEGERIAN & ASSOCIATES, INC (310) 860 0770 AMehrzadi@Shegerianlaw.com Attorney for Plaintiff, ELIZABETH MATUSEK-BLEECKER
Kevin P. Lee, Esq., JACKSON LEWIS P.C. (415) 394-9400 Kevin.Lee@jacksonlewis.com Attorney for Defendants, SURGICAL CARE AFFILIATES, LLC and MARY FLYNN

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS SURGICAL CARE AFFILIATES, LLC, AND MARY FLYNN's Amended DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Defendants' demurrer to two counts of the complaint - (7) Labor Code 1102.5 and (9) intentional infliction of emotional distress (IIED) - is sustained with leave to amend. As to Labor Code 1102.5, "facts in support of each of the requirements of a statute upon which a cause of action is based must be specifically pled." (Fisher v. San Pedro Peninsula Hospital (1989) 214 Cal.App.3d 590, 604.) Here, the "protected complaint" specifically pled was to two "fellow registered nurses" (Cmplt. 6:3-11), but section 1102.5 requires disclosure "to a person with authority over the employee or another employee who has the authority to investigate" (Id.) As to the IIED claim, the pled conduct must be "extreme and outrageous." (Hughes v. Pair (2009) 46 Cal.4th 1035, 1050.) Plaintiff cites no authority holding that conduct of the sort pled here is extreme and outrageous.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
